programs.name: NC Medicaid Long-Term Care
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage including long-term care services such as nursing home care, assisted living, and home-based supports for low-income seniors. It covers non-medical services to help frail seniors remain in their homes when possible.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ who are low-income, meet asset limits (e.g., $2,000 for individual), U.S. citizens or qualified non-citizens, and NC residents needing long-term care.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Institutional care; home care up to limits like $1,330/month income eligibility; nursing home level of care.[1]

programs.name: NC Medicaid Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waivers
programs.what_it_does: Offers waiver programs to provide home and community-based services like personal care, adult day care, and assisted living to delay nursing home placement. Slots are limited with possible waiting lists, unlike entitlement programs.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors 65+ eligible for nursing home level of care, low-income/asset limits (e.g., $1,330/month income, $2,000 assets).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Home-based services equivalent to nursing home care; income limit $1,330/month, asset $2,000.[1]

programs.name: NC Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Combines Medicaid and Medicare benefits into one program providing comprehensive long-term care, including medical, social services, dental, and eye care. Delivered through multidisciplinary teams to keep participants community-based.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level of care, eligible for both Medicare and Medicaid, living in PACE service areas.[1]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive Medicare/Medicaid LTC services plus extras like dental/eye care.

programs.name: NC Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: State Medicaid programs that help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B premiums/costs; SLMB/QI cover Part B premiums.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare enrollees 65+ meeting NC income/asset limits (varies by program; e.g., QI up to 135% FPL).[7]
programs.benefit_value: Premium/cost-sharing assistance; e.g., Part B premium ~$174.70/month (2024 rates, subject to change).

programs.name: NC Food and Nutrition Services (FNS)/SNAP
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing food at approved retailers. Administered by county DSS to combat hunger.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ with income below SNAP limits (~130% FPL), assets under $4,250 (higher if 60+).[5]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+/month per person depending on household size/income.

programs.name: NC Low-Income Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers help with home heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance. Includes one-time payments to energy providers.[4][5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+ at or below 150% FPL, priority to elderly/disabled.[4]
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,000 one-time payment depending on fuel type/household.

programs.name: NC Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs. Reduces energy costs and improves safety.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters 60+ meeting income guidelines (200% FPL), priority seniors/disabled.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free upgrades worth thousands; long-term energy savings.

programs.name: NC Senior Health Insurance Information Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, Medigap, Medicare Advantage, and help with billing issues. Helps seniors choose/understand options.[5]
programs.who_its_for: All NC Medicare beneficiaries 65+, spouses, younger disabled; no income limit.[5]

programs.name: NC Home-Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often 5+ meals/week through Area Agencies on Aging.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or at nutrition risk.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Suggested $3-5 donation/meal; free for lowest income.

programs.name: NC Lifespan Respite Program / Family Caregiver Support
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite breaks for caregivers of seniors via vouchers for paid relief care. Includes caregiver training and support services.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of frail seniors 60+ or adults 18-59 with Alzheimer’s; income ≤$35K individual.[5]

programs.name: NC Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service jobs with minimum wage pay and trains seniors for unsubsidized employment. Promotes workforce re-entry.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ unable to find employment.[5]
programs.benefit_value: 20 hrs/week at min. wage (~$7.25/hr); job training.

programs.name: NC Legal Assistance for Older Adults
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for issues like benefits access, housing, abuse prevention, and consumer problems. Through legal aid organizations.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing legal barriers to independence.[5]

programs.name: NC Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigates complaints, and ensures quality of life/rights protection. Operates statewide network.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Residents/families in NC nursing homes, assisted living, adult care homes.[5]

programs.name: North Carolina Homestead Exemption
programs.what_it_does: This property tax relief program reduces the annual tax burden for qualifying seniors by exempting a portion of their home's assessed value from property taxes. It provides financial relief to help low-income elderly homeowners remain in their residences.
programs.who_its_for: Residents aged 65 or older who meet county-specific income thresholds.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific property tax exemption tailored to NC seniors with varying income limits by county, beyond standard federal benefits.
programs.benefit_value: Reduction in property taxes; amount varies by county and home value (e.g., up to $38,000 exemption in some areas)[1]

programs.name: North Carolina Long-Term Care Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: This program covers skilled nursing care and long-term care services under different eligibility rules than standard Medicaid, allowing middle-income seniors to qualify with proper planning. It helps pay for nursing home or in-home care that can cost $10,000-$15,000 monthly.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors requiring long-term care, including those with assets who can qualify through planning; medical expenses exceeding income may aid eligibility.
programs.whats_unique: NC-administered Medicaid waiver with state-specific rules for asset protection and planning, distinct from federal baseline Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Covers nursing home costs up to $10,000-$15,000/month; potential monthly aid up to $3,500 if medical expenses exceed income[1]

programs.name: Project C.A.R.E.
programs.what_it_does: This community-based program provides care management and alternatives to institutionalization for frail older adults. It coordinates services to support aging in place through home and community-based options.
programs.who_its_for: Frail older adults aged 60+ at risk of nursing home placement.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded NC program under Division of Aging focused on preventing institutionalization via targeted care management.
programs.benefit_value: Case management and service coordination; value varies by needs (e.g., home care, adult day services)[5]

programs.name: North Carolina Seniors Farmers' Market Nutrition Program
programs.what_it_does: This program provides vouchers for seniors to purchase fresh produce at farmers' markets. It promotes nutrition and health among low-income older adults.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific nutrition voucher program unique to NC, partnering with local farmers' markets.
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers worth $20-$50 annually for fresh produce[5]

programs.name: State-County Special Assistance - In Home
programs.what_it_does: This program delivers state-county funded in-home assistance to low-income seniors. It supports activities of daily living to enable independent living at home.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income adults aged 60+ needing in-home personal care.
programs.whats_unique: NC's state-county partnership program providing cash or in-kind home care beyond federal SSI/SSP.
programs.benefit_value: Monthly assistance payments or services; up to state maximum (e.g., $500+/month varying by county)[5]
